commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3af7624d2f0785839901e17625c583d0f463ac7d)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a shared helper that maps `.docodex`, `.pptcodex`, and `.xlscodex` to Office extensions, applies it to agent file-tool path normalization and workspace guards, and adds unit tests.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +150. Total +184 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the execution-side gap: current main leaves a supplied `.docodex` path unchanged through XML-only path normalization. The model-side rewrite remains intermittent and was not live-reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the canonical reject-vs-correct semantics and require contributor real behavior proof; this is not a safe autonomous repair until that policy is confirmed.

target.title: fix(agents): auto-correct hallucinated docx/pptx/xlsx extensions in tool path params
target.body:
## Summary
Auto-correct model-hallucinated document file extensions (`.docodex` → `.docx`, `.pptcodex` → `.pptx`, `.xlscodex` → `.xlsx`) at the shared agent-tool parameter boundary. The correction is applied to `path` parameters in file tools (read/write/edit) before filesystem execution, alongside the existing XML suffix normalization pipeline.

Fixes #93326

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Model-supplied paths like `report.docodex` pass unchanged through the current path-normalization pipeline, causing file-operation failures. Existing path cleanup only strips malformed XML suffixes (`</arg_value>>`) and does not handle hallucinated document extensions.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13.1, Linux
- **Code analysis path**: `src/agents/agent-tools.params.ts` → `stripMalformedXmlArgValueSuffixFromKeys` / `replaceKnownHallucinatedExtension`, `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts` → sandbox read path / `createOpenClawReadTool`
- **Test framework**: Vitest

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.params.test.ts
pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.read.arg-value-suffix.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.read.workspace-root-guard.test.ts
pnpm format:check src/agents/agent-tools.params.ts src/agents/agent-tools.params.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  28 passed (28)
   Start at  10:16:32
   Duration  819ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
   Start at  10:07:20
   Duration  8.99s

All matched files use the correct format.
```

**Observed result after the fix**: Path parameters ending in `.docodex` (or `.pptcodex`/`.xlscodex`) are transparently corrected to their canonical extensions before reaching file tool execution. Valid extensions pass through unchanged. The correction composes correctly with existing XML suffix stripping (e.g., `data.xlscodex</arg_value>>` → `data.xlsx`).

**What was not tested**: Live model reproduction of the `.docodex` hallucination (the model-side trigger is intermittent and not deterministically reproducible in this checkout). The execution-side gap — where a hallucinated path reaching the tool boundary passes through unchanged — is covered and verified by unit tests.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ | 28 passed in agent-tools.params.test.ts (11 new extension correction tests); 18 passed in read tool tests |
| Type Check | ✅ | `pnpm check:test-types` exit code 0 |
| Lint | ✅ | `pnpm format:check` clean on all 3 changed files |

## Risk checklist
**Did user-visible behavior change?** `No` — only hallucinated nonexistent extensions are auto-corrected; valid file paths with real extensions pass through unchanged.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No`

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No` — the correction only rewrites the path parameter value; filesystem sandboxing and path guards remain in effect.

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested
target.author: zhangqueping
